content.problemStatement:
There is a building with NNN floors (numbered 111 through NNN from bottom to top); on each floor, there are MMM windows (numbered 111 through MMM from left to right). Let's denote the jjj-th window on the iii-th floor by (i,j)(i, j)(i,j).

All windows in the building need to be cleaned one by one in a specific order. You are given this order as a matrix AAA with NNN rows and MMM columns; for each valid iii and jjj, the window (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) must be cleaned on the AN‚àíi+1,jA_{N-i+1,j}AN‚àíi+1,j‚Äã-th turn.

Whenever we clean a window (i,j)(i, j)(i,j), it becomes clean, but the dirty water used for cleaning it flows down to windows (i‚àí1,j‚àí1)(i-1, j-1)(i‚àí1,j‚àí1), (i‚àí1,j)(i-1, j)(i‚àí1,j) and (i‚àí1,j+1)(i-1, j+1)(i‚àí1,j+1) (more precisely, to all of these windows which exist), so these windows become dirty again. Then, the water flows further down, so the same applies for other windows below: whenever a window (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) becomes dirty, windows (i‚àí1,j‚àí1)(i-1, j-1)(i‚àí1,j‚àí1), (i‚àí1,j)(i-1, j)(i‚àí1,j) and (i‚àí1,j+1)(i-1, j+1)(i‚àí1,j+1) become dirty as well. For example, cleaning a window (5,3)(5, 3)(5,3) will make the window (3,2)(3, 2)(3,2) dirty. The next window is cleaned only after water flows down completely. Note that each window is cleaned only once and there may be dirty windows at the end.

For each window, determine whether it will be clean after the cleaning process ends.
constraints: 1‚â§Ai,j‚â§N‚ãÖM1 \le A_{i, j} \le N \cdot M1‚â§Ai,j‚Äã‚â§N‚ãÖM for each valid i,ji, ji,j
the elements of the matrix AAA are pairwise distinct
the sum of N‚ãÖMN \cdot MN‚ãÖM over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of N‚ãÖMN \cdot MN‚ãÖM over all test cases does not exceed 625625625
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains MMM space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,‚Ä¶,Ai,MA_{i, 1}, A_{i, 2}, \ldots, A_{i, M}Ai,1‚Äã,Ai,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ai,M‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print NNN lines; each of them should contain a string with length MMM. For each valid iii and jjj, the jjj-th character on the iii-th line should be '1' if the window (N‚àíi+1,j)(N-i+1, j)(N‚àíi+1,j) is clean or '0' if it is dirty.
